---
title: "figure1_summary"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/8/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(writexl)
```


```{r}
helios_core_old = read_excel("data/HELIOS_Core_redacted.xlsx")
helios_core_new = read.csv("data/HELIOS2_Core_redacted_300.csv")
helios_core_new$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "CHINESE", "C",
                                             ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "INDIAN", "I",
                                             ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "MALAY", "M",
                                             ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "Other", "O", NA))))
helios_core_new$FREG7_Gender <- ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG7_Gender == "male", "M",
                                       ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG7_Gender == "female", "F",NA))
                                             

#helios_core_old
old_all = helios_core_old[,c("FREG0_PID", "FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "FREG7_Gender", "FREG8_Age", "DBI13_Height", "DBI14_Weight", "FWH16_Waist1", "FWH19_Hip1", "FBP16_Caffeine", "FBP17_Smoking", "DBP9_Sbp_1", "DBP12_Dbp_1","DBP15_Hr_1")]
new_all = helios_core_new[,c("FREG0_PID", "FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "FREG7_Gender", "FREG8_Age", "DBI13_Height", "DBI14_Weight", "FWH16_Waist1", "FWH19_Hip1", "FBP16_Caffeine", "FBP17_Smoking", "DBP9_SBP_1","DBP12_DBP_1", "DBP15_HR_1")]

```
"FREG0_PID", "FREG1_Barcode"

```{r}
library(writexl)

colnames(old_all) <- tolower(colnames(old_all))
colnames(new_all) <- tolower(colnames(new_all))

helios800 = rbind(old_all, new_all)
helios800
#write_xlsx(helios800, "data/helios800.xlsx")
```
```{r}
metadata_micro = read_csv("data/metadata_fil_01032024_redacted.csv")
sample_list = metadata_micro$`Original Sample name`
helios_new800 <- filter(helios800, freg1_barcode %in% sample_list)
helios_new800
```


```{r}
helios_new800$bmi = helios_new800$dbi14_weight/(helios_new800$dbi13_height*0.01)^2
helios_new800$bmi_groups <- cut(helios_new800$bmi, breaks = c(0,18.5,23,25,Inf),
                  labels = c("Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", "Obese"),
                  include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE)
helios_new800
#write_xlsx(helios_new800, "data/summary_800.xlsx")

```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Define custom ethnic colors and single colors for other variables
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887", "O" = "#80796BFF")
variable_colors <- c(
  "age" = "#B19CD9",        # Purple for age
  "bmi" = "#FFB6C1",        # Light pink for BMI
  "waist" = "#FFE4B5",      # Light orange for waist
  "hip" = "#90EE90",        # Light green for hip
  "dbp" = "#ADD8E6",        # Light blue for DBP
  "sbp" = "#FFB366",        # Orange for SBP
  "hr" = "#D3D3D3"          # Light gray for HR
)

# Function to create individual-level bar plots
create_individual_plot <- function(data, var_name, use_ethnic_colors = FALSE) {
  base_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1)) +
    labs(x = "Participants", 
         y = paste0(gsub("_", " ", var_name)),
         title = gsub("_", " ", var_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10)
    )
    
  if (use_ethnic_colors) {
    base_plot + 
      geom_col(aes(fill = freg5_ethnic_group), width = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)
  } else {
    var_type <- case_when(
      grepl("age", var_name) ~ "age",
      grepl("bmi", var_name) ~ "bmi",
      grepl("waist", var_name) ~ "waist",
      grepl("hip", var_name) ~ "hip",
      grepl("dbp_1$", var_name) ~ "dbp",
      grepl("sbp", var_name) ~ "sbp",
      grepl("hr", var_name) ~ "hr",
      TRUE ~ "default"
    )
    
    if(var_name == "y") {  # For actual measurements
      base_plot + 
        geom_col(aes(y = .data[[var_name]]), width = 1, fill = variable_colors[var_type])
    } else {  # For gender
      base_plot + 
        geom_col(aes(fill = freg7_gender), width = 1) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c("male" = "#0072B2", "female" = "#CC79A7"))
    }
  }
}

# Helper function to get units for variables
get_unit <- function(var_name) {
  units <- list(
    freg8_age = "years",
    bmi = "kg/m^2",
    fwh16_waist1 = "cm",
    fwh19_hip1 = "cm",
    dbp9_sbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp12_dbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp15_hr_1 = "bpm"
  )
  return(units[[var_name]] %||% "")
}

# Function to create all individual plots
create_all_individual_plots <- function(data) {
  # Sort data by ethnic group
  data <- data %>% arrange(freg5_ethnic_group)
  
  # Create list of plots
  plots <- list(
    ethnicity_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg5_ethnic_group", use_ethnic_colors = TRUE),
    age_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg8_age"),
    bmi_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "bmi"),
    waist_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh16_waist1"),
    hip_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh19_hip1"),
    dbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp12_dbp_1"),
    sbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp9_sbp_1"),
    hr_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp15_hr_1")
  )
  
  # Create gender plot with colors
  plots$gender_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1, fill = freg7_gender)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("male" = "#0072B2", "female" = "#CC79A7")) +
    labs(x = "Participants", y = "", title = "Gender") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10)
    )
  
  return(plots)
}

# Example usage:
# First make sure your data is properly sorted
helios_new800 <- helios_new800 %>% arrange(freg5_ethnic_group)

# Create all plots
plots <- create_all_individual_plots(helios_new800)

# To combine all plots vertically:
library(patchwork)
combined_plot <- plots$ethnicity_plot / plots$age_plot / plots$bmi_plot / 
                plots$waist_plot / plots$hip_plot / plots$dbp_plot / 
                plots$sbp_plot / plots$hr_plot / plots$gender_plot +
                plot_layout(heights = rep(1, 9))

# Save the combined plot
#ggsave("figure/combined_plots.png", combined_plot, height = 22, width = 10)
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Define custom ethnic colors and single colors for other variables
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887", "O" = "#80796BFF")
gender_colors <- c("F" = "#CC79A7", "M" = "#0072B2")  # Define specific gender colors
variable_colors <- c(
  "age" = "#B19CD9",        # Purple for age
  "bmi" = "#FFB6C1",        # Light pink for BMI
  "waist" = "#FFE4B5",      # Light orange for waist
  "hip" = "#90EE90",        # Light green for hip
  "dbp" = "#ADD8E6",        # Light blue for DBP
  "sbp" = "#FFB366",        # Orange for SBP
  "hr" = "#D3D3D3"          # Light gray for HR
)

# Function to create individual-level bar plots
create_individual_plot <- function(data, var_name, use_ethnic_colors = FALSE) {
  if (use_ethnic_colors) {
    # For ethnicity plot
    p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1, fill = freg5_ethnic_group)) +
      geom_col(width = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)
  } else if (var_name == "gender") {
    # For gender plot with fixed height and categorical colors
    p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1, fill = freg7_gender)) +
      geom_col(width = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = gender_colors)
  } else {
    # For all other variables
    p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = .data[[var_name]])) +
      geom_col(width = 1, fill = case_when(
        var_name == "freg8_age" ~ variable_colors["age"],
        var_name == "bmi" ~ variable_colors["bmi"],
        var_name == "fwh16_waist1" ~ variable_colors["waist"],
        var_name == "fwh19_hip1" ~ variable_colors["hip"],
        var_name == "dbp12_dbp_1" ~ variable_colors["dbp"],
        var_name == "dbp9_sbp_1" ~ variable_colors["sbp"],
        var_name == "dbp15_hr_1" ~ variable_colors["hr"]
      ))
  }

  # Add common theme elements
  p + labs(x = "Participants", 
         y = paste0(gsub("_", " ", var_name), " (", get_unit(var_name), ")"),
         title = gsub("_", " ", var_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10)
    ) +
    if(var_name == "gender" || use_ethnic_colors) {
      # Remove y-axis text for categorical plots
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())
    } else {
      theme()
    }
}

# Helper function to get units for variables
get_unit <- function(var_name) {
  units <- list(
    freg8_age = "years",
    bmi = "kg/m^2",
    fwh16_waist1 = "cm",
    fwh19_hip1 = "cm",
    dbp9_sbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp12_dbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp15_hr_1 = "bpm"
  )
  return(units[[var_name]] %||% "")
}

# Function to create all individual plots
create_all_individual_plots <- function(data) {
  # Sort data by ethnic group
  data <- data %>% 
    arrange(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
    # Ensure gender is F/M format
    mutate(freg7_gender = toupper(substr(freg7_gender, 1, 1)))
  
  # Create list of plots
  plots <- list(
    ethnicity_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg5_ethnic_group", use_ethnic_colors = TRUE),
    age_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg8_age"),
    bmi_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "bmi"),
    waist_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh16_waist1"),
    hip_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh19_hip1"),
    dbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp12_dbp_1"),
    sbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp9_sbp_1"),
    hr_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp15_hr_1"),
    gender_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "gender")
  )
  
  return(plots)
}

# Example usage:
# First make sure your data is properly sorted
helios_new800 <- helios_new800 %>% arrange(freg5_ethnic_group)

# Create all plots
plots <- create_all_individual_plots(helios_new800)

# To combine all plots vertically:
library(patchwork)
combined_plot <- plots$ethnicity_plot / plots$age_plot / plots$bmi_plot / 
                plots$waist_plot / plots$hip_plot / plots$dbp_plot / 
                plots$sbp_plot / plots$hr_plot / plots$gender_plot +
                plot_layout(heights = rep(1, 9))

# Save the combined plot
#ggsave("figure/combined_plots.png", combined_plot, height = 22, width = 10)
```

```{r}
plot_info <- list(
  list(name = "ethnicity_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/ethnicity_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "age_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/age_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "bmi_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/bmi_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "waist_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/waist_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "hip_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/hip_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "dbp_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/dbp_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "sbp_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/sbp_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "hr_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/hr_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "gender_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/gender_distribution.pdf")
)

# Save individual plots as PDF (vector) files
for (plot_item in plot_info) {
  ggsave(
    filename = plot_item$filename,
    plot = plots[[plot_item$name]],
    width = 10,
    height = 3,
    device = cairo_pdf  # Explicitly use cairo_pdf for high-quality vector output
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gghalves)

# Define the same colors as before for consistency
variable_colors <- c(
  "age" = "#B19CD9",        # Purple for age
  "bmi" = "#FFB6C1",        # Light pink for BMI
  "waist" = "#FFE4B5",      # Light orange for waist
  "hip" = "#90EE90",        # Light green for hip
  "dbp" = "#ADD8E6",        # Light blue for DBP
  "sbp" = "#FFB366",        # Orange for SBP
  "hr" = "#D3D3D3"          # Light gray for HR
)

# Create individual half violin + half box plots
# Age plot
plot_age <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "Age", y = freg8_age)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["age"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["age"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Age (years)") +
  theme_minimal()

# BMI plot
plot_bmi <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "BMI", y = bmi)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["bmi"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["bmi"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "BMI (kg/m²)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Waist plot
plot_waist <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "Waist", y = fwh16_waist1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["waist"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["waist"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Waist (cm)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Hip plot
plot_hip <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "Hip", y = fwh19_hip1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["hip"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["hip"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Hip (cm)") +
  theme_minimal()

# SBP plot
plot_sbp <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "SBP", y = dbp9_sbp_1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["sbp"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["sbp"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "SBP (mmHg)") +
  theme_minimal()

# DBP plot
plot_dbp <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "DBP", y = dbp12_dbp_1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["dbp"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["dbp"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "DBP (mmHg)") +
  theme_minimal()

# HR plot
plot_hr <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "HR", y = dbp15_hr_1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["hr"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["hr"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "HR (bpm)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Combine all plots using patchwork
library(patchwork)
combined_violin_plots <- plot_bmi + plot_age +  plot_waist + 
                        plot_hip + plot_sbp + plot_dbp + 
                        plot_hr +
                        plot_layout(ncol = 7, widths = rep(1, 7))


ggsave("figure/figure1_summary/combined_violin_plots.pdf", combined_violin_plots, 
       width = 15, height = 6)
```

## life style
```{r}
helios_life = read_excel("data/summary_800.xlsx")
helios_life
```

```{r}
# For caffeine consumption by ethnic group
ggplot(helios_life %>% 
       filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)), # Remove NA from ethnic groups
       aes(x = freg5_ethnic_group, fill = fbp16_caffeine)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Ethnic Group", 
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Caffeine Consumption",
       title = "Caffeine Consumption by Ethnic Group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues")

# For smoking frequency by ethnic group 
ggplot(helios_life %>% 
       filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)), # Remove NA from ethnic groups
       aes(x = freg5_ethnic_group, fill = fbp17_smoking)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Ethnic Group", 
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Smoking Frequency",
       title = "Smoking Frequency by Ethnic Group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Oranges")
```
```{r}
# For proportional representation
ggplot(helios_life, aes(x = freg5_ethnic_group, fill = fbp16_caffeine)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(x = "Ethnic Group", 
       y = "Percentage",
       fill = "Caffeine Consumption",
       title = "Proportion of Caffeine Consumption by Ethnic Group") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create proportions and prepare data
plot_data <- helios_life %>%
  filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group, fbp16_caffeine) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
  mutate(prop = count/sum(count),
         # Create variables for donut chart positioning
         ymax = cumsum(prop),
         ymin = c(0, head(ymax, n=-1)),
         labelpos = (ymax + ymin)/2) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create the donut charts with faceting
ggplot(plot_data, aes(fill = fbp16_caffeine, ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  geom_rect(aes(ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  facet_wrap(~freg5_ethnic_group, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Caffeine Consumption Distribution by Ethnic Group",
       fill = "Caffeine Consumption") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# For smoking data, similar approach:
plot_data_smoking <- helios_life %>%
  filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group, fbp17_smoking) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
  mutate(prop = count/sum(count),
         ymax = cumsum(prop),
         ymin = c(0, head(ymax, n=-1)),
         labelpos = (ymax + ymin)/2) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create donut charts for smoking
ggplot(plot_data_smoking, aes(fill = fbp17_smoking, ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  geom_rect(aes(ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  facet_wrap(~freg5_ethnic_group, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Oranges",
                    labels = c("Smoker", "Non-smoker")) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Smoking Distribution by Ethnic Group",
       fill = "Smoking") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
